summary.coverage_notes: All acceptance criteria from tasks.json are covered. Two edge cases (TC-038 degenerate clamp, TC-039 non-numeric localStorage) are not covered by existing tests — TC-039 is filed as BUG-002.

failed_tests.test: executeAgentRun > shows 'Opening terminal...' toast and error when terminalSender is null after 500ms wait
failed_tests.note: Confirmed present on main branch before feature branch was cut. Out of scope for this QA cycle.

performance.notes: Not applicable. Feature is a pure client-side UI interaction with no network calls. mousemove handler performs O(1) arithmetic + one localStorage.setItem per event. No performance threshold risk.

owasp_checks.name: Broken Access Control
owasp_checks.notes: No access control surface. Feature is client-side UI only.

owasp_checks.name: Cryptographic Failures
owasp_checks.notes: No sensitive data stored. localStorage key holds a numeric panel width only.

owasp_checks.name: Injection
owasp_checks.notes: useLocalStorage reads stored value with JSON.parse wrapped in try/catch. A non-numeric value (e.g. string 'abc') would survive JSON.parse as a string type. Math.max/Math.min with a string coerce it to NaN. clamp(NaN) returns NaN. style.width becomes 'NaNpx', making the panel invisible. Filed as BUG-002 (Medium).

owasp_checks.name: Insecure Design
owasp_checks.notes: Design follows ADR-1. Width clamped to [minWidth, maxWidth] on every render.

owasp_checks.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
owasp_checks.notes: localStorage is same-origin. No integrity risk beyond the NaN issue noted under A03.

owasp_checks.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
owasp_checks.notes: No logging requirement for a client-side resize interaction.

owasp_checks.name: Server-Side Request Forgery
owasp_checks.notes: No network requests made by this feature.

test_cases.description: Returns defaultWidth when localStorage is empty
Returns stored width when a valid value exists
Clamps stored value below minWidth to minWidth
Clamps stored value above maxWidth to maxWidth
Returns exact minWidth when stored value equals minWidth
Returns exact maxWidth when stored value equals maxWidth
Forwards minWidth and maxWidth in return value
Dragging left increases width
Dragging right decreases width
Width clamped to minWidth during drag
Width clamped to maxWidth during drag
Multiple mousemove events accumulate from startWidth
Width written to localStorage on each mousemove
Width persists across re-render
mousemove after mouseup does not change width
Unmount mid-drag does not throw
Unmount removes mousemove and mouseup from window
ConfigPanel renders drag handle with role=separator
ConfigPanel aside has style.width 480px by default
ConfigPanel aside does not have w-[480px] class
AgentSettingsPanel renders drag handle with role=separator
AgentSettingsPanel aside has style.width 480px by default
AgentSettingsPanel aside does not have w-[480px] class
Drag handle is first child of aside in both panels
test_cases.notes: Verified by code inspection — separator div rendered before header div

test_cases.description: aside has relative Tailwind class in both panels
Default width 480px on first load
Width persists in localStorage under correct key per panel
test_cases.notes: storageKey values confirmed in code: prism:panel-width:config and prism:panel-width:agent-settings

test_cases.description: StorageKey isolation — panels have independent keys
test_cases.notes: Distinct keys confirmed by code inspection

test_cases.description: localStorage key namespaced and stores only a number
test_cases.notes: setStoredWidth called with a clamped number only

test_cases.description: Drag handle emits no user-controlled content to DOM
test_cases.notes: No innerHTML usage in hook or handle element

test_cases.description: OWASP A03 — JSON.parse of localStorage value falls back gracefully
test_cases.notes: try/catch in useLocalStorage handles parse failure. However non-numeric (string) values survive parse and produce NaN width. See BUG-002.

test_cases.description: TypeScript compilation — zero errors
w-[480px] class removed from both components
test_cases.notes: grep confirmed not present in className strings

test_cases.description: Drag handle ARIA: aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax present
test_cases.notes: All three attributes present in both components

test_cases.description: Drag handle has no keyboard interaction
test_cases.notes: role=separator implies keyboard resize via arrow keys per WAI-ARIA. No onKeyDown implemented. See advisory note in bugs.md.

test_cases.description: style={{ width }} on aside violates CLAUDE.md no-inline-styles rule
useCallback dep array includes width — handler recreated on each resize
test_cases.notes: Functionally correct. handleMouseDown captures current width to compute delta from startWidth correctly.

test_cases.description: Degenerate clamp range: minWidth === maxWidth
test_cases.notes: Not tested. Behavior is safe (returns min). Coverage gap but no bug.

test_cases.description: Non-numeric localStorage value produces NaNpx width
